message:
fix(package_manager): preserve lockfile when settings.json is missing

Review-pass follow-up on 5f82141e. `read_settings_json` returns
`Ok({})` for a missing file (intentional, to let callers treat
"no config yet" as "defaults"). But `reconcile_lockfile_sync` fed
that empty object through `list_packages_in_settings`, which produced
an empty live set — and then pruned every entry in the lockfile on
the assumption that "no live packages" meant "everything is stale".

That's exactly backwards. A missing settings file is "no
authoritative info", not "zero packages". The fix is to check
`settings_path.exists()` up front and skip reconciliation for the
scope when it's absent, so an accidental deletion or a fresh clone
without settings doesn't silently wipe the provenance/trust/digest
data in `packages.lock.json`.

Regression test `reconcile_lockfile_preserves_entries_when_settings_file_is_missing`
seeds a real lockfile, removes the settings file, reconciles, and
asserts the lockfile is preserved with `0` prunes reported.
